summary: Structured gap analysis and concrete remedies to reach practical 'left brain' parity. Includes logic depth, numeric robustness, integer enforcement, adaptive refinement, and optional MILP fallback.
capabilities_now: Deterministic planning with quantified 'all' implications and clause generation
Linear constraints solving with tight bounds via Z3 Optimize
Extended envelopes: bilinear, squares, log, sigmoid, piecewise
Bilinear branch-and-bound envelope refinement on segments
Integer variables enforced in bound tightening and projection
Adaptive segmentation on violation telemetry in Executive
Robust symbolic grounding and quantified alternations (forall–exists, exists–forall)
CPU-only quantifier elimination with multi-arity predicates and per-assignment Skolemization
Typed A* planning with numeric gating and principled refusal
Envelope-based handling of squares, logs, sigmoids, bilinear, piecewise with adaptive segmentation
Mixed-integer tags honored in Z3/box/MILP fallback paths for tighter bounds
Self-evolving architecture with one-shot training via induction and confidence/telemetry gating for robust updates
Adaptive envelope segmentation range 6–12; hardened default 10
Randomized multi-run stress harness validates latency distribution; telemetry captures p95 runtime and violation rate
Extreme stress harness on 30 variables with mixed envelopes validates refusal and latency
Convergence labeling 'Convergence (Δμ < ε)' using μ=ρ/(1+χ); ε configurable
Principled refusal triggers on type mismatches and infeasible envelopes within bounded time

gaps.current: Basic quantified forms and simple nested 'forall' handled
gaps.target: Deeper quantified clause generation and resolution across nested implications
actions: Generalize ClauseGenerator for multiple quantifiers and alternations
Integrate resolution of generated clauses into Executive planning step
Add tests for nested quantifiers and mixed predicates
gaps.core_issue: Nested quantified reasoning increases clause space combinatorially; some forms are undecidable. Requires Skolemization/CNF normalization and controlled resolution depth under CPU-only constraints.

gaps.current: Nonconvex handled via envelopes + segmentation; no exact global solving
gaps.target: Optional MILP/MINLP bounds when PuLP/OR-Tools present; fallback safe to Z3
actions: Use PuLP/OR-Tools for linear integer bounds where available
Retain Z3 Optimize fallback for portability
Add environment checks and telemetry on solver choice
gaps.core_issue: Global nonconvex MINLP is NP-hard; Z3 is not a global optimizer. Envelope relaxations provide robust approximations, not exact optima. Exact global solvers require specialized libraries and potentially heavy compute.

gaps.current: Integers detected at ingestion; enforced in optimize and projection
gaps.target: Ensure integer consistency wherever numeric checks occur
actions: Propagate int_vars through Executive numeric paths
Add tests for integer-only feasibility and mixed integer cases
gaps.core_issue: Mixed Int/Real reasoning can introduce coercion and rounding artifacts. Consistent propagation and enforcement across ingestion, planning, and solver calls is required to avoid infeasible projections.

gaps.current: Segmentation increased on projection violations; piecewise densifies by curvature
gaps.target: Refine only where contradictions persist; telemetry-guided segmentation
actions: Record violations and re-solve with increased segments on implicated variables
Piecewise densification near steep slope changes
Add tests verifying refinement resolves contradictions
gaps.core_issue: Local refinement improves tightness but can blow up runtime if applied broadly. Densification is sensitive to sample quality; needs telemetry to target segments with contradictions.

gaps.current: Basic typed relation schemas attached at ingestion
gaps.target: Prefer type-consistent edges and unify with typed variables in planning
actions: Enhance Executive typemap and edge selection based on type match
Add tests for type-guided path preference
gaps.core_issue: Absent unified ontology and strict typing can lead to over/under-constrained unification. Edge selection must respect types to prevent semantic drift during planning.

gaps.target: Expand policies for principled refusals and alignment
actions: Add fine-grained refusal conditions tied to numeric infeasibility
Expose telemetry hooks to value system
gaps.core_issue: Policy semantics require careful design to avoid brittle refusals. Telemetry-driven hooks must be aligned with value system goals without creating unintended blocks.

telemetry.milp_solver: auto: PuLP/OR-Tools if available; else Z3
telemetry.refusal_policy: enabled for type violations and infeasible envelopes
